Title: Implement Search Functionality for Issuers and Documents   As a user I want to search for issuers and documents So that I can easily find and interact with them   Acceptance Criteria:   Search Page:   Implement a search functionality that allows users to search for issuers and documents. Display the search results on a new page. Search Results:   Ensure the search results include both issuers and documents matching the search criteria. Display the relevant details for each search result item. Interactions with Search Results:   Enable interactions with items from the search results page (e.g., requesting a document). Maintain the state of interactions across different views of the application. Status Updates:   If a document is requested from the search results page, the status of the document (e.g., in progress, request completed, or failed) should be updated and reflected across all views where the document appears. Ensure that the updated status is visible when the user views the document from any part of the application, including the search results page and other document lists. Consistency:   Ensure the user experience is consistent and seamless, with no need for users to manually refresh or navigate away to see the updated status of their requests. Provide visual feedback on the status of the document request in real-time. Error Handling:   Implement proper error handling for search operations and document requests. Provide informative messages to users in case of failures during search or request operations.

There is two type of Access Token which gets shared by DDA Token API –               1. Node 6 Access Token               2. Trusted X Access Token (Old Implementation)   As of now the token generation mechanism got changed to Node 6 in all the cases (Like Login process – Mobile app end) except the Share Access token which gets retrieved from Mobile App and get shared with SP during the share flow. Currently, we have faced few issues on the validation of the access token from SP side because of configuration from DDA end which didn’t allowed to do validation on Trusted X tokens.. There must be some configuration changes maintained at DDA end for the SP client id to allow trusted X token to get verified.   To avoid further issues with it, as the Node 6 token is now stable enough, request you to include the below Product enhancement item in the WSR as suggested by Najjar.   Enhancement Item: Change the procurement of Share Access token by UAEPASS DV App from TrustedX to Node 6 to enhance the performance in sharing flow and to unify the token verification process at SP end.   Note: DV backend pass this share access token received form UAE PASSS DV Mobile App to SP service (pass through) during sharing presentation.

Title: Show Progress Bar After Document Request As a user I want to see the progress of my document request after pressing the request button So that I can track the status of my document request until it is completed Acceptance Criteria: After pressing the request button, a progress bar is displayed to show the updated status of the document request.The progress bar must accurately reflect the current status of the document request regardless of the page or filter selected.The progress bar should persist and display the correct status even if the user navigates to different pages or changes document filters.Once the document request is completed, the progress bar updates to indicate completion and the document status is updated accordingly in the list.

Title: Document Request with Real-Time Status Update   As a user I want to request a document and see the status update in real-time So that I can track the progress of my document request without leaving the page   Acceptance Criteria:   Document Request:   Allow users to request a document from any relevant view in the application. Display a progress bar immediately after the request is initiated. Real-Time Status Update:   Show real-time status updates of the document request on the progress bar. Ensure that the status updates are accurate and reflect the current state (e.g., in progress, request completed, or failed). Consistency Across Views:   Maintain the status of the document request across all views in the application. Ensure that if the user navigates away and returns to the document, the progress bar reflects the current status. Error Handling:   Provide clear error messages if the document request fails.

Ensure Proper App Lifecycle Handling and Resource Management As a product team member, I want to investigate the app's lifecycle behavior, so that we can ensure that the app handles backgrounding, resuming, and other lifecycle events without issues, preventing any memory leaks or resource handling issues. Acceptance Criteria: App Lifecycle Scenarios: Investigate the behavior of the app when it is moved to the background and reopened after an extended period. Ensure that the app does not get stuck or freeze when resumed from the background. Memory Management: Analyze and verify that there are no memory leaks occurring during app lifecycle transitions. Confirm that the app is releasing unused resources correctly during transitions, including backgrounding, pausing, and termination events. Resource Management: Ensure proper handling and release of resources, such as network connections and cached data, when the app is backgrounded or closed. Testing and Reporting: Conduct tests for different app lifecycle scenarios across iOS and Android. Provide a report summarizing the findings and propose any necessary fixes to ensure smooth handling of lifecycle events.

Detailed User Flow: First Document Addition After Profile UpgradeObjective: Guide users who have upgraded from SOP 1 to SOP 3 through the process of adding their first document to the Digital Vault, and encourage SOP 3 users who have not yet utilized this feature.Flow Overview: Post-Upgrade Highlight:After a successful profile upgrade from SOP 1 to SOP 3, the "Documents" tab is highlighted to notify users that the Digital Vault is now enabled and available.The goal is to draw attention to the newly unlocked feature and prompt users to explore the vault.Target Audience:Newly Upgraded Users: SOP 1 to SOP 3 users seeing the Digital Vault for the first time.Existing SOP 3 Users: Users who have never added a document, excluding those who have added documents via the home page "Add Documents" option or through a sharing request.Step-by-Step Guided Journey:Step 1: Start the Journey - Upon entering the "Documents" tab for the first time, a prompt encourages users to start the document addition journey.Step 2: Press "Request a Document" - Users are guided to click on the "Request a Document" button.Step 3: Select "Request" for Emirates ID - The journey directs users to request their Emirates ID, making it an easy first document to add.Step 4: Progress Bar Completion - Users see the progress bar indicating the document retrieval process.Step 5: View the Document - Once the request is complete, users are prompted to view the newly added document.Step 6: Press "View Document" Button - This action takes the user to the document details screen.Step 7: Success Screen - A congratulatory message appears, celebrating the successful addition of their first document to the Digital Vault.Step 8: Return to the Documents Tab - Users are navigated back to the Documents tab, where they can see their newly added Emirates ID and are encouraged to explore adding other documents.Expected Outcomes: Increased User Engagement: By highlighting the Documents tab and guiding users through their first addition, we anticipate increased interaction with the Digital Vault.Enhanced Onboarding Experience: This step-by-step guide ensures users understand the process and feel a sense of achievement after adding their first document.Higher Adoption Rates: Encouraging existing SOP 3 users to utilize the vault will help maximize the value of the Digital Vault feature.

New Story : reduction of Redis adapter service

to reduce the usage of Redis Adapter Service. Because the purpose of second level cache is not getting served through this microservice. The I/O is quite high compare to DB call. So my proposal would be to use Redis Adapter Service only for those cases where DV is not storing/ owning the data at DV DB i.e., Storing CitizenAuth object at Redis during Login API call and Storing Verifiable Credential, Verifiable Presentation Object(s) at Redis during Data-Check. User Story: Detection of Eligible Users Title: Detect Eligible Users for First Document Addition Journey Description: As a system, I want to identify users who have upgraded from SOP 1 to SOP 3 and existing SOP 3 users who have not yet added any documents to their Digital Vault, so that they can be guided through the first document addition journey. Acceptance Criteria: Identify SOP 1 to SOP 3 users who have completed the profile upgrade.Detect existing SOP 3 users who have not added any documents.Exclude users who have added documents via "Add Documents" from the home page or through a sharing request.Ensure the list of eligible users is accurately maintained and updated.

User Story: Create the Guided Document Addition Journey Title: Guided Journey for First Document Addition Description: As an eligible user, I want to be guided through the process of adding my first document to the Digital Vault, so that I can easily understand and use this feature. Acceptance Criteria: Step 1: Prompt the user to start the guided journey when visiting the Documents tab.Step 2: Guide the user to press the "Request a Document" button.Step 3: Direct the user to request their Emirates ID.Step 4: Display a progress bar to show the document retrieval process.Step 5: Prompt the user to view the document upon successful retrieval.Step 6: Show a "Congratulations" message once the document is added.Step 7: Navigate the user back to the Documents tab where they can see their newly added document and have the option to request more.

User Story: Push Notification for Encouraging Document Addition in Digital Vault Title: Send Push Notifications to Encourage Document Addition for Eligible Users Description: As a system, I want to send push notifications to users who have not yet added any documents to their Digital Vault, so that they are encouraged to use the feature and add their documents. Acceptance Criteria: User Detection:Identify users who have upgraded to SOP 3 but have not added any documents to their Digital Vault.Exclude users who have previously interacted with the “Add Documents” feature from the home page or through a sharing request.Push Notification Logic:Send push notifications periodically to eligible users to remind and encourage them to add their first document.The frequency of notifications should be configurable (e.g., every month, every two months).Ensure notifications are engaging and provide a clear call to action.User Interaction:When the user clicks on the push notification, navigate them directly to the Documents tab in the app.Highlight or guide the user on how to start the document addition journey.Configuration & Tracking:Admins should have the ability to configure the notification frequency from the backend.Track user engagement metrics from the push notifications, such as click-through rates and subsequent actions.

User Authentication As a user, I want to authenticate via UAE Pass so that I can securely access the application.As a user, I want the app to manage my session so that I don’t have to log in repeatedly.As a user, I want the app to refresh my authentication token automatically so that I remain logged in without manual intervention.As a user, I want to receive error messages when authentication fails so that I understand the issue and take corrective action.Document Request & Sharing Flow As a service provider, I want to request specific documents from a user so that I can obtain the necessary information securely.As a user, I want to receive a notification when a document request is made so that I am aware and can take action.As a user, I want to review and approve or reject a document request via UAE Pass so that I have control over my shared documents.As a user, I want the app to retrieve and display my approved documents so that I can verify what was shared.As a service provider, I want to receive a notification if a document request is rejected so that I can take appropriate follow-up actions.QR Code Generation & Usage As a service provider, I want to generate a QR code so that users can scan it and initiate a document-sharing request.As a user, I want to scan a QR code using UAE Pass so that I can quickly authenticate and approve document requests.As a user, I want to see a confirmation after scanning the QR code and approving a request so that I know my document was successfully shared.As a service provider, I want the QR code scanning process to trigger the appropriate UAE Pass API calls so that the document-sharing request is processed correctly.Security Protocols As a user, I want my sensitive data to be encrypted so that my documents remain secure.As a user, I want the application to use secure API communication so that my authentication and document transactions are protected from unauthorized access.As a service provider, I want the system to enforce access control policies so that only authorized users can access requested documents.Cross-Platform Support As a user, I want the app to function seamlessly on both iOS and Android so that I can use it regardless of my device.As a user, I want a consistent experience across platforms so that I can easily navigate and use the application.Request ID Generation As a service provider, I want the application to automatically generate a unique request ID so that I can track and manage document requests efficiently.

Developing in One Sprint and Testing in the Next Is Not Scrum and it is causing some issues

When development and testing are split across two Sprints, stakeholders experience reduced visibility into the team's progress. They must wait longer to see what the Scrum team is working on, hindering collaboration and feedback. Furthermore, this approach decreases predictability as teams are effectively planning two Sprints' worth of work during Sprint Planning. Coordinating development and testing efforts across two Sprints can become burdensome, making it challenging to meet Sprint goals consistently.

Scrum's incremental approach grants teams the flexibility to change direction easily as they work on small, manageable increments of functionality. When development and testing are separated across two Sprints, the team accumulates a substantial amount of "half-baked" work. Consequently, it becomes challenging to pivot or respond to changing requirements swiftly. To change direction, the team must wait until both the development and testing phases are completed, which can take at least two Sprints.

The Agile Manifesto's very first principle emphasizes "early and continuous delivery of valuable software" as a key principle for Agile teams. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next introduces unnecessary delays in delivering value to the customer. If you are developing functionality in one Sprint and testing in the next, that means that it will take your Scrum Team at least two Sprint to deliver functionality to the customer, rather than one.

The 2020 Scrum Guide highlights that Scrum aims to control risk through an iterative, incremental approach. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next increases the risk substantially. It postpones feedback from customers and stakeholders potentially leading to increased investment in the development of features that customers may not find useful. Additionally, it make take the Scrum team two Sprints - instead of one - to learn that planned functionality is not technically feasible. This can result in wasted effort and resources.

Separating development and testing into distinct Sprints introduces unnecessary complexity into the process. Teams must track which features have been developed but not tested beyond the scope of a single Sprint. Questions arise about whether separate product backlog items are needed for development and testing or how these items should be tied together. The lack of clear definitions can lead to confusion and inefficiency. It becomes difficult to gauge when development is truly complete.
Developing and Testing in the One Sprint will resolve these issues
Ideas on how we can achieve this
Feature Toggles: Use feature toggles to merge code into the main branch without impacting the functionality. This allows QA to test features even when they are not fully complete.
TDD/BDD: Adopt these practices to ensure that testing is an integral part of the development process, not an afterthought.
Test Automation: Automate repetitive and regression testing to save time and improve efficiency.
Deployment Automation: Automate the deployment process to facilitate smoother handovers between teams.
Cross-skilling: Encourage team members to learn skills across roles. This can include having developers learn basic testing techniques and QA team members understanding the development workflow.

Subject: Transaction & Digital Signature Discussion with DV Team   Topic: Meeting conducted between TDRA and DDA team to discuss Transaction & Digital Signature Discussion

MoM sent by DDA Team:   Dear @Abdalla AlNoman,   Thanks for your time today   Below are the discussion points and inputs required from DV team:   Transaction History Feature: - DDA recommended consolidating transaction details for document sharing into the "Transaction History" tab, alongside login and sign details, to provide users with a comprehensive overview of their transactions. - DDA proposed relocating the "Transaction History" tab to the notification area and shifting notifications to the top of the interface, similar to other applications, as it will notify users about their transactions. - DDA will share the final design after DV alignment and agreement on the above points   Digital Signature Feature: - DV team will assess how to manage the storage of bulk signed documents - DDA will arrange a meeting to present the prototype   Maitha

Dear Maitha, Hope this email finds you well.   Following my last email, please find below our initial comments and concerns on the Transaction history design, as we see it will have an impact on DV features:   Concerns • Major change in user’s mental model to adjust the Notifications tab, which is core to accessing DV functionality / sharing requests – testing • Moving notifications to a bell at the top doesn’t work for functionality where you need to action something that’s been pushed to you • Transaction history name is misleading as it implies something financial – Activity log, Interactions, etc. would probably make more sense as the name • What happens to the Archived section, News?   More info needed • Benchmarks and User research results • A/B testing is needed if we want to replace the Notifications tab with Transactions History   Suggestions • Transaction History can be added in the profile tab instead • Rename Transaction History to Activity Log   Abdalla   As per our last meeting’s request we have compiled our DV transactions to be included as part of Transaction history, we raised it to management review. We will share the list as soon as we get feedback.

Title: Implement Search Functionality for Issuers and Documents   As a user I want to search for issuers and documents So that I can easily find and interact with them   Acceptance Criteria:   Search Page:   Implement a search functionality that allows users to search for issuers and documents. Display the search results on a new page. Search Results:   Ensure the search results include both issuers and documents matching the search criteria. Display the relevant details for each search result item. Interactions with Search Results:   Enable interactions with items from the search results page (e.g., requesting a document). Maintain the state of interactions across different views of the application. Status Updates:   If a document is requested from the search results page, the status of the document (e.g., in progress, request completed, or failed) should be updated and reflected across all views where the document appears. Ensure that the updated status is visible when the user views the document from any part of the application, including the search results page and other document lists. Consistency:   Ensure the user experience is consistent and seamless, with no need for users to manually refresh or navigate away to see the updated status of their requests. Provide visual feedback on the status of the document request in real-time. Error Handling:   Implement proper error handling for search operations and document requests. Provide informative messages to users in case of failures during search or request operations.

There is two type of Access Token which gets shared by DDA Token API –               1. Node 6 Access Token               2. Trusted X Access Token (Old Implementation)   As of now the token generation mechanism got changed to Node 6 in all the cases (Like Login process – Mobile app end) except the Share Access token which gets retrieved from Mobile App and get shared with SP during the share flow. Currently, we have faced few issues on the validation of the access token from SP side because of configuration from DDA end which didn’t allowed to do validation on Trusted X tokens.. There must be some configuration changes maintained at DDA end for the SP client id to allow trusted X token to get verified.   To avoid further issues with it, as the Node 6 token is now stable enough, request you to include the below Product enhancement item in the WSR as suggested by Najjar.   Enhancement Item: Change the procurement of Share Access token by UAEPASS DV App from TrustedX to Node 6 to enhance the performance in sharing flow and to unify the token verification process at SP end.   Note: DV backend pass this share access token received form UAE PASSS DV Mobile App to SP service (pass through) during sharing presentation.

Ensure Proper App Lifecycle Handling and Resource Management As a product team member, I want to investigate the app's lifecycle behavior, so that we can ensure that the app handles backgrounding, resuming, and other lifecycle events without issues, preventing any memory leaks or resource handling issues. Acceptance Criteria: App Lifecycle Scenarios: Investigate the behavior of the app when it is moved to the background and reopened after an extended period. Ensure that the app does not get stuck or freeze when resumed from the background. Memory Management: Analyze and verify that there are no memory leaks occurring during app lifecycle transitions. Confirm that the app is releasing unused resources correctly during transitions, including backgrounding, pausing, and termination events. Resource Management: Ensure proper handling and release of resources, such as network connections and cached data, when the app is backgrounded or closed. Testing and Reporting: Conduct tests for different app lifecycle scenarios across iOS and Android. Provide a report summarizing the findings and propose any necessary fixes to ensure smooth handling of lifecycle events.

Detailed User Flow: First Document Addition After Profile UpgradeObjective: Guide users who have upgraded from SOP 1 to SOP 3 through the process of adding their first document to the Digital Vault, and encourage SOP 3 users who have not yet utilized this feature.Flow Overview: Post-Upgrade Highlight:After a successful profile upgrade from SOP 1 to SOP 3, the "Documents" tab is highlighted to notify users that the Digital Vault is now enabled and available.The goal is to draw attention to the newly unlocked feature and prompt users to explore the vault.Target Audience:Newly Upgraded Users: SOP 1 to SOP 3 users seeing the Digital Vault for the first time.Existing SOP 3 Users: Users who have never added a document, excluding those who have added documents via the home page "Add Documents" option or through a sharing request.Step-by-Step Guided Journey:Step 1: Start the Journey - Upon entering the "Documents" tab for the first time, a prompt encourages users to start the document addition journey.Step 2: Press "Request a Document" - Users are guided to click on the "Request a Document" button.Step 3: Select "Request" for Emirates ID - The journey directs users to request their Emirates ID, making it an easy first document to add.Step 4: Progress Bar Completion - Users see the progress bar indicating the document retrieval process.Step 5: View the Document - Once the request is complete, users are prompted to view the newly added document.Step 6: Press "View Document" Button - This action takes the user to the document details screen.Step 7: Success Screen - A congratulatory message appears, celebrating the successful addition of their first document to the Digital Vault.Step 8: Return to the Documents Tab - Users are navigated back to the Documents tab, where they can see their newly added Emirates ID and are encouraged to explore adding other documents.Expected Outcomes: Increased User Engagement: By highlighting the Documents tab and guiding users through their first addition, we anticipate increased interaction with the Digital Vault.Enhanced Onboarding Experience: This step-by-step guide ensures users understand the process and feel a sense of achievement after adding their first document.Higher Adoption Rates: Encouraging existing SOP 3 users to utilize the vault will help maximize the value of the Digital Vault feature.

User Story: Detection of Eligible Users Title: Detect Eligible Users for First Document Addition Journey Description: As a system, I want to identify users who have upgraded from SOP 1 to SOP 3 and existing SOP 3 users who have not yet added any documents to their Digital Vault, so that they can be guided through the first document addition journey. Acceptance Criteria: Identify SOP 1 to SOP 3 users who have completed the profile upgrade.Detect existing SOP 3 users who have not added any documents.Exclude users who have added documents via "Add Documents" from the home page or through a sharing request.Ensure the list of eligible users is accurately maintained and updated.

User Story: Create the Guided Document Addition Journey Title: Guided Journey for First Document Addition Description: As an eligible user, I want to be guided through the process of adding my first document to the Digital Vault, so that I can easily understand and use this feature. Acceptance Criteria: Step 1: Prompt the user to start the guided journey when visiting the Documents tab.Step 2: Guide the user to press the "Request a Document" button.Step 3: Direct the user to request their Emirates ID.Step 4: Display a progress bar to show the document retrieval process.Step 5: Prompt the user to view the document upon successful retrieval.Step 6: Show a "Congratulations" message once the document is added.Step 7: Navigate the user back to the Documents tab where they can see their newly added document and have the option to request more.

User Story: Push Notification for Encouraging Document Addition in Digital Vault Title: Send Push Notifications to Encourage Document Addition for Eligible Users Description: As a system, I want to send push notifications to users who have not yet added any documents to their Digital Vault, so that they are encouraged to use the feature and add their documents. Acceptance Criteria: User Detection:Identify users who have upgraded to SOP 3 but have not added any documents to their Digital Vault.Exclude users who have previously interacted with the “Add Documents” feature from the home page or through a sharing request.Push Notification Logic:Send push notifications periodically to eligible users to remind and encourage them to add their first document.The frequency of notifications should be configurable (e.g., every month, every two months).Ensure notifications are engaging and provide a clear call to action.User Interaction:When the user clicks on the push notification, navigate them directly to the Documents tab in the app.Highlight or guide the user on how to start the document addition journey.Configuration & Tracking:Admins should have the ability to configure the notification frequency from the backend.Track user engagement metrics from the push notifications, such as click-through rates and subsequent actions.

User Story: Handling error scenarios / retry

User Authentication As a user, I want to authenticate via UAE Pass so that I can securely access the application.As a user, I want the app to manage my session so that I don’t have to log in repeatedly.As a user, I want the app to refresh my authentication token automatically so that I remain logged in without manual intervention.As a user, I want to receive error messages when authentication fails so that I understand the issue and take corrective action.Document Request & Sharing Flow As a service provider, I want to request specific documents from a user so that I can obtain the necessary information securely.As a user, I want to receive a notification when a document request is made so that I am aware and can take action.As a user, I want to review and approve or reject a document request via UAE Pass so that I have control over my shared documents.As a user, I want the app to retrieve and display my approved documents so that I can verify what was shared.As a service provider, I want to receive a notification if a document request is rejected so that I can take appropriate follow-up actions.QR Code Generation & Usage As a service provider, I want to generate a QR code so that users can scan it and initiate a document-sharing request.As a user, I want to scan a QR code using UAE Pass so that I can quickly authenticate and approve document requests.As a user, I want to see a confirmation after scanning the QR code and approving a request so that I know my document was successfully shared.As a service provider, I want the QR code scanning process to trigger the appropriate UAE Pass API calls so that the document-sharing request is processed correctly.Security Protocols As a user, I want my sensitive data to be encrypted so that my documents remain secure.As a user, I want the application to use secure API communication so that my authentication and document transactions are protected from unauthorized access.As a service provider, I want the system to enforce access control policies so that only authorized users can access requested documents.Cross-Platform Support As a user, I want the app to function seamlessly on both iOS and Android so that I can use it regardless of my device.As a user, I want a consistent experience across platforms so that I can easily navigate and use the application.Request ID Generation As a service provider, I want the application to automatically generate a unique request ID so that I can track and manage document requests efficiently.

DVT-392 - Rate limiting to processing messages in batch job

As a member of the DTP Ops Team, I can configure parallel threads in batch where the Issuer Type / SP priority can be controlled using a database table and the evidence/visualization request is called from issuers in a round-robin fashion, so that we are able to ensure SPs can receive documents from sharing requests faster and more successfully. Acceptance criteria Evidence/Visualization requests should be fulfilled per the proposed solution (Option 1 in the attached slides): Fetch all pending evidence/visualization request where service is up. Excluding the credentials which are partially shared and whose time slot has been exceeded and the corresponding issuer is in active status.Segregate the Issuer as per the document type. Each bucket contains number of threads based on document types.Run concurrent thread per Issuer doctype. And set a maximum issue call so, we can throttle the HTTP call.Call the issuer and send the evidence/visualization request in round robin fashion. So that no one service provider will  have to wait for in-finite number of time.

Reference Remedy Ticket number : INC000006841034 As per FAB-NHL "The existing API response is not sufficient to differentiate issue types and its long process for us to flag the issue types whether it related to system or user." FAB-NHL Team wants to get an additional response field which will help them understand the reason behind why a request was not successfully shared or why is it stuck in No Action Taken/ Saved for Later/ Failed states.  The response field can mention them that the user needs to upgrade their certificates to be able to continue with sharing, the user does not have the necessary documents, the sharing request has failed due to issue faced at the Issuing Entity Systems, etc.   Please find attached the MoM attached for the discussion that happened with the DV Dev Team and FAB-NHL Team. column4: In the new design system, there is no bold style to be used and we are instead shading the colour of the notification darker

column2: Next action for expired and cannot be shared can be made clear
column3: Design team to check and provide input if changes are needed (done)No further action
column4: We are already addressing this as part of the Revamp (the message copy becomes: 'BankCo doesn’t accept this expired document. Please contact your issuer.'  We have not included it in the Reskin because it requires using the SP's name and therefore adds a small amount of complexity. If we want to address it in the Reskin, we will also need to update the message for Expired and Accepted documents to have the message: 'BankCo accepts this expired document'

column2: Deselect for optional docs is an additional step
column3: Design team to check and provide input if changes are needed (done)No further action
column4: Addressed in the Revamp; we proposed to use checkboxes to allow for single-step deselection but this was not implemented for the Reskin due to complexity

column2: Hide docs which are not requested in selection screen for predefined
column3: Design team to check and provide input if changes are needed (done)No further action
column4: Addressed in the Revamp (the user does not access the selection screen for predefined documents); the Reskin behaviour matches current production behaviour. If the document is predefined and available, it should be automapped and if it is not available, the user should be prompted to add it, so the user shouldn't need to access the selection screen. The empty state design should be as per: [figma link|https://www.figma.com/file/Ns5wCexZR1VkDYo5fO7BvX/DTP_for-review?type=design&node-id=9425-47078&mode=design&t=qJ4SxWcXjNCjdmF1-4.] We do agree with the comment - that a user should only see the instance of the document the SP is requesting - but given the above (and particularly that it is addressed in the Revamp), it is not a priority to address.
